methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $335M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Nuclear Energy

data.narrative:
Tokamak Energy is a Series C nuclear energy company founded by David Kingham and Mikhail Gryaznevich that has raised $335M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 11 peers across nuclear energy: 3 public companies (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private firms including Oklo, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Nano Nuclear Energy.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables in the narrow nuclear energy set—only 2 adjacent-stage peers were identified, below the threshold of 3 required for reliable median benchmarking. Accordingly, the analysis relies on stage-progression methodology, applying a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement to the $335M total raised proxy, yielding an implied valuation of $570M.

The triangulated analysis, constrained to a single applicable method, supports a range of $484M to $655M with a median point of $570M. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 1.7x multiplier for this specific sector—where capital intensity and regulatory trajectory differ materially from venture-backed software or hardware cohorts—and the absence of direct same-stage peer pricing. A more robust assessment would require either additional private comparable data at equivalent stage and scale, or observable pricing from nuclear energy transactions occurring post-Series B.
